description_long: This object is a long-handled broom with a rectangular, slightly tapered base that houses the bristle section. The handle is slender, straight, and appears to be made of a light-colored material, likely wood or plastic, extending vertically from the center of the broom head. The broom head is blocky and angular, with a flat bottom and a wider base that narrows slightly toward the handle connection. The color scheme consists of a tan or light brown head and a pale, almost white handle. There are no visible bristles extending from the base, suggesting a stylized or simplified design. No brand markings, labels, or decorative elements are present on any visible surface.
description: A long-handled broom with a tan, blocky head and a straight, pale handle. The design is simple and lacks visible bristles or branding.
description_view_0: The broom is seen from an angled side, showing the straight handle and the angular, tapered broom head.
description_view_1: A direct front view highlights the symmetrical, flat face of the broom head and the centered handle.
description_view_2: This angled rear view reveals the opposite side of the broom head, emphasizing its blocky, geometric shape.
description_view_3: A direct rear view displays the back face of the broom head and the straight handle attachment.
attribute_handle_type: The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of light-colored wood or plastic with a round cross-section. No ergonomic grips, telescoping features, or end caps are visible.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: No visible bristles are present; the broom head is a solid, blocky piece with no discernible bristle pattern or arrangement.
attribute_mounting_position: The broom is likely intended to be stored vertically, leaned against a wall, as there are no visible holes or hooks for hanging.
